Class RobotController

java.lang.Object
RobotController
public class RobotController extends Object
The game rule engine: decides whether moves are legal and executes commands.

The controller sits between the UI (RobotGame) and the domain objects (Grid, Robot). The UI turns keyboard input into Commands and asks the controller to execute them. The controller answers: "Is this move legal?" and "What should happen next?" It checks the grid (bounds and rocks) before telling the robot to move, so all game rules live here instead of in the UI or the robot.

  • Constructor Details

    • RobotController

      public RobotController(Grid grid, Robot robot)
      Creates a controller for the given grid and robot. Validates that the robot starts inside the grid and not on a rock; throws if the setup is invalid so bugs are caught early.
      Parameters:
      grid - the game board
      robot - the robot (must be placed inside the grid and not on a rock)
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the robot is outside the grid or on a rock

  • Method Details

    • runScript

      public void runScript(List<Command> commands)
      Runs a list of commands one after another and prints each step to the console. Useful for demos or automated tests without the graphical UI.
      Parameters:
      commands - the sequence of commands to execute

    • execute

      public CommandResult execute(Command command)
      Executes one command (move forward, turn left, or turn right) and returns the result. For moves, the controller checks the next cell first; if it is off the grid or has a rock, the move is rejected and a message is returned without changing the robot.
      Parameters:
      command - the command to execute
      Returns:
      a result describing whether the command succeeded and what message to show the player

    • hasReachedGoal

      public boolean hasReachedGoal()
      Returns whether the robot is currently on the goal (charging station) cell.
      Returns:
      true if the robot has reached the goal

    • getRobot

      public Robot getRobot()
      Returns the robot managed by this controller.
      Returns:
      the robot

    • getGrid

      public Grid getGrid()
      Returns the grid managed by this controller.
      Returns:
      the grid
